task_completion.description: InVesalius 3 is open with the CT Cranium dataset loaded.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The InVesalius 3 main window is visible, displaying medical imaging slices (axial, coronal, sagittal) and/or a 3D model.

task_completion.description: The agent opened the export dialog for 2D slices.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box for exporting images or 2D slices is open within the InVesalius application.

task_completion.description: Axial orientation is selected for export.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the export dialog, the 'Axial' option is selected as the slice orientation to export.

task_completion.description: TIFF format is selected for the exported images.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the export dialog, the file type or format dropdown is set to TIFF (or .tif).

task_completion.description: The destination directory is set to /home/ga/Documents/axial_tiff_stack/.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The save location in the export dialog or file chooser is clearly set to '/home/ga/Documents/axial_tiff_stack/'.

task_completion.description: The export process is completed and files are saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A progress bar completing the export, or a file manager/terminal window showing the newly created .tif files in the target directory.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the InVesalius GUI to perform the export.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the mouse cursor interacting with InVesalius menus, dialogs, and buttons, rather than just running a script in the terminal.

integrity.description: The exported files were generated by InVesalius, not mocked.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent did not use terminal commands like 'touch' or 'cp' to create fake .tif files in the destination directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset contains exactly 108 axial slices. The output directory must be '/home/ga/Documents/axial_tiff_stack'. The exported files must be valid TIFF images (.tif) larger than 10 KB. A successful export will result in exactly 108 files in the target directory.
